questions.question: Evaluate $\int x^3 e^x \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Let $u = x^3$, $dv = e^x dx$. Then $du = 3x^2 dx$, $v = e^x$. $\int x^3 e^x dx = x^3 e^x - 3\int x^2 e^x dx$. Using $\int x^2 e^x dx = e^x(x^2 - 2x + 2)$ from Quiz 1, we get $x^3 e^x - 3e^x(x^2 - 2x + 2) + C = e^x(x^3 - 3x^2 + 6x - 6) + C$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int e^{3x} \cos(2x) \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Let $I = \int e^{3x}\cos(2x)dx$. Use parts twice, solving the system: $I = \frac{e^{3x}}{13}(2\sin 2x + 3\cos 2x) + C$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int \frac{\ln x}{x^5} \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Let $u = \ln x$, $dv = x^{-5}dx$. Then $du = \frac{1}{x}dx$, $v = -\frac{1}{4}x^{-4}$. $\int \frac{\ln x}{x^5}dx = -\frac{\ln x}{4x^4} + \frac{1}{4}\int x^{-5}dx = -\frac{\ln x}{4x^4} - \frac{1}{16x^4} + C = -\frac{4\ln x + 1}{16x^4} + C$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int x^2 \arctan x \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Let $u = \arctan x$, $dv = x^2 dx$. Then $du = \frac{1}{1+x^2}dx$, $v = \frac{x^3}{3}$. $\int x^2\arctan x dx = \frac{x^3}{3}\arctan x - \frac{1}{3}\int \frac{x^3}{1+x^2}dx$. Since $\frac{x^3}{1+x^2} = x - \frac{x}{1+x^2}$, the integral becomes $\frac{x^3}{3}\arctan x - \frac{1}{3}(\frac{x^2}{2} - \frac{1}{2}\ln(1+x^2)) + C = \frac{x^3}{3}\arctan x - \frac{x^2}{6} + \frac{1}{6}\ln(1+x^2) + C$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int \arcsin(3x) \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Let $u = \arcsin(3x)$, $dv = dx$. Then $du = \frac{3}{\sqrt{1-9x^2}}dx$, $v = x$. $\int \arcsin(3x)dx = x\arcsin(3x) - 3\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-9x^2}}dx$. Substitute $w = 1-9x^2$, $dw = -18x dx$, giving $\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-9x^2}}dx = -\frac{1}{9}\sqrt{1-9x^2}$. Thus result = $x\arcsin(3x) + \frac{1}{3}\sqrt{1-9x^2} + C$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int x^5 \ln x \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Let $u = \ln x$, $dv = x^5 dx$. Then $du = \frac{1}{x}dx$, $v = \frac{x^6}{6}$. $\int x^5 \ln x dx = \frac{x^6}{6}\ln x - \frac{1}{6}\int x^5 dx = \frac{x^6}{6}\ln x - \frac{x^6}{36} + C$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int \sin(\ln x) \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Substitute $t = \ln x$, $x = e^t$, $dx = e^t dt$. Then $\int \sin(\ln x) dx = \int e^t \sin t \, dt = \frac{e^t}{2}(\sin t - \cos t) + C = \frac{x}{2}(\sin(\ln x) - \cos(\ln x)) + C$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int \sec^3 x \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Use integration by parts: $u = \sec x$, $dv = \sec^2 x dx$. Then $du = \sec x \tan x dx$, $v = \tan x$. After manipulation, $2\int \sec^3 x dx = \sec x \tan x + \ln|\sec x + \tan x| + C$, so the result is $\frac{1}{2}\sec x \tan x + \frac{1}{2}\ln|\sec x + \tan x| + C$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\int e^{2x} \sin 3x \, dx$.
questions.explanation: Let $I = \int e^{2x}\sin 3x dx$. Use integration by parts twice to obtain $I = \frac{e^{2x}}{13}(2\sin 3x - 3\cos 3x) + C$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered advanced integration by parts.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review cyclic integrals and trigonometric integrals.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on choosing $u$ and $dv$ correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Review the integration by parts formula and standard integrals.
